title: balsamorhiza hookeri var. incana
intro: Balsamorhiza hookeri var. incana, also known as Hooker's balsamroot, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is a variety of the Balsamorhiza hookeri plant, known for its yellow or orange flowers and edible roots, which are rich in medicinal properties. Traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, the roots and leaves of Hooker's balsamroot have been employed to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive problems, and soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the roots are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, while the leaves can be used fresh in salads or as a medicinal infusion.
